body.text: There is not in them any disposition to forgive;
they do not forgive any one a sin, a crime,
They have not a disposition to excuse nor a disposition to forgive;
they do not excuse nor do they forgive a sin, a crime,
(TA.) The rájiz (Sakhr-el-Ghei, L) said, (on seeing his companions, with whom he was journeying, fly from the Benu-l-Mustalik, whom they chanced to meet, L,)

yaA qawomi layosato fiyhimu gafiyraho
                      
                     
                        faA@mo$uwA kamaA tamo$iY jimaAlu AlHiyraho
O my people, there is not in them any disposition to forgive: therefore march ye as march the camels of El-Heereh
]: (S, L:) he mentioned particularly the camels of El-Heereh because they carry burdens; and meant, march ye heavily, and defend yourselves, and do not fly. (L.)       -A2-  Also
in family and cattle or other property. (TA, from a trad.)       -A3-  See also
